REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER REGINALD HINDLEY'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON AMENDED PETITION TO MODIFY OR VACATE FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD IS GRANTED. PURSUANT TO THE COURT OF APPEAL DECISION IN ACTION CPF-14-513720 THE COURT, NOT THE ARBITRATOR, WAS TO DETERMINE THE SCOPE OF THE INJUNCTION AND IS TO DETERMINE WHETHER THE INJUNCTION SHOULD BE LIFTED. IN LIGHT OF THIS DECISION, THE COURT SHALL VACATE THE ARBITRATION AWARD, EVEN THOUGH BOTH THE TRIAL COURT AND THE COURT OF APPEALS CONFIRMED THAT, AT LEAST ON THE ISSUE OF THE SCOPE OF THE INJUNCTION, THE ARBITRATOR WAS CORRECT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; REPORTER JANE HONG, CSR 11975, 909-831-2644, CSRJANE@HOTMAIL.COM. (D501)

Notice Of Hearing On Amended Petition To Modify Or Vacate Final Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Apr-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Phil Foster Esq., 415-626-8189, in-person for respondent, Semetc LLC and Mark Epstein
Mark Clausen Esq., 415-707-235-3663, via zoom for petitioner

Mr. Foster contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PETITIONER REGINALD HINDLEY'S NOTICE OF HEARING ON AMENDED PETITION TO MODIFY OR VACATE FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD is GRANTED. Pursuant to the Court of Appeal decision in action CPF-14-513720 the Court, not the arbitrator, was to determine the scope of the injunction and is to determine whether the injunction should be lifted. In light of this decision, the Court shall vacate the arbitration award, even though both the trial Court and the Court of Appeals confirmed that, at least on the issue of the scope of the injunction, the arbitrator was correct.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Reporter Jane Hong, CSR 11975, 909-831-2644, CSRJane@hotmail.com. (D501)
